name: Apples - Sliced
recipeIngredient: An average of 19 pounds for a canner load of 7 quarts
An average of 12-1/4 pounds for a canner load of 9 pints
A bushel (48 pounds) yields approximately 16 to 19 quarts, or about 2-3/4 pounds per quart
recipeInstructions:
1. Select juicy, crispy apples that are both sweet and tart.
2. Wash the apples, then peel and core them.
3. Slice the apples into water containing ascorbic acid to prevent discoloration.
4. For hot packs only: Place drained slices in a large saucepan and add 1 pint of water or very light/light/medium syrup (see "Preparing and Using Syrups") per 5 pounds of sliced apples.
5. Boil for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally to avoid burning.
6. Fill jars with hot slices and hot syrup or water, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
7. Adjust lids and process according to the appropriate table based on canning method (boiling-water, dial-gauge pressure, or weighted-gauge pressure) and altitude.
Tables:
Table 1: Recommended processing time for sliced apples in a boiling-water canner
- Jar Size: Pints or Quarts
- Altitude: 0 - 1,000 ft: 20 minutes; 1,001 - 3,000 ft: 25 minutes; 3,001 - 6,000 ft: 30 minutes; Above 6,000 ft: 35 minutes
Table 2: Recommended processing time and pressure for sliced apples in a dial-gauge pressure canner
- Jar Size: Pints or Quarts
- Altitude: 0 - 2,000 ft: 6 lb; 2,001 - 4,000 ft: 7 lb; 4,001 - 6,000 ft: 8 lb; Above 6,000 ft: 9 lb
Table 3: Recommended processing time and pressure for sliced apples in a weighted-gauge pressure canner
- Jar Size: Pints or Quarts
- Altitude: 0 - 1,000 ft: 5 lb; Above 1,000 ft: 10 lb
